Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1: Search Airtable for Jordan Ekwueme's records using airtable_search_records (query: "Jordan Ekwueme" or "BrightLoop"). Find: (a) Relocation record recReloc00000008 is erroneously marked Status: "Completed", explaining the housing processing issue; (b) Stipend record rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand has a Review Note stating "Administrative hold. Do not process." Further investigation through Slack and email reveals Marcus Thorne placed the hold pending forensic clearance from engineering.
2: Search Slack for the payment block details using conversations_search_messages (query: "Jordan" or "stipend" or "Marcus"). Find Marcus Thorne's message in the #finance channel (channel ID: C005) stating he will not release the funds until he sees hard technical proof from engineering that the receipt is a false positive.
3: Search Slack or Emails for engineering context using conversations_search_messages or search_emails (query: "receipt", "scanner", "engineering", "duplicate"). Find Dmitri Volkov's message announcing the receipt metadata scanner and stating that manual verification results are logged in the tracking issue.
4: Search Linear for the audit ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "duplicate", "stipend", "scanner", or similar). Find the tracking ticket ENG-220 ("Stipend duplicate detection").
5: Read Linear issue comments to find the forensic proof using linear_list_comments (issue_id: "ENG-220" or "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection"). Find Dmitri Volkov's comment confirming Jordan Ekwueme's receipt SHA-256 hash is unique, classifying it as a false positive.
6: Identify the vendor for "The Hive" using search_emails (query: "The Hive") or quickbooks_search_vendors. Find Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Corporate Housing (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com).
7: Update Jordan's Airtable relocation record to fix the processing error using airtable_update_records (record id: recReloc00000008, field: Status updated to "In Progress" or similar active state).
8: Escalate the payment block to Marcus Thorne in Slack using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005" or Marcus's DM). The message must provide the technical proof (SHA-256 hash or forensic confirmation) found in Linear to clear the hold.
9: Reach out to the vendor to salvage the reservation using send_email (sender: "suki.patel@moveops.com", recipients: "carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com", subject and body requesting to reinstate or salvage The Hive booking).
10: Reply to Jordan using send_email (sender: "suki.patel@moveops.com", recipients: "jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com", subject and body assuring him his housing logistics are secure, the hotel is salvaged, and explaining the stipend hold was an audit false positive that is now cleared).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
1: Search Airtable for Jordan Ekwueme's records using airtable_search_records (query: "Jordan Ekwueme" or "BrightLoop"). Find: (a) Relocation record recReloc00000008 is erroneously marked Status: "Completed", explaining the housing processing issue; (b) Stipend record rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand has a Review Note stating "Administrative hold. Do not process." Further investigation through Slack and email reveals Marcus Thorne placed the hold pending forensic clearance from engineering.
2: Search Slack for the payment block details using conversations_search_messages (query: "Jordan" or "stipend" or "Marcus"). Find Marcus Thorne's message in the #finance channel (channel ID: C005) stating he will not release the funds until he sees hard technical proof from engineering that the receipt is a false positive.
3: Search Slack or Emails for engineering context using conversations_search_messages or search_emails (query: "receipt", "scanner", "engineering", "duplicate"). Find Dmitri Volkov's message announcing the receipt metadata scanner and stating that manual verification results are logged in the tracking issue.
4: Search Linear for the audit ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "duplicate", "stipend", "scanner", or similar). Find the tracking ticket ENG-220 ("Stipend duplicate detection").
5: Read Linear issue comments to find the forensic proof using linear_list_comments (issue_id: "ENG-220" or "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection"). Find Dmitri Volkov's comment confirming Jordan Ekwueme's receipt SHA-256 hash is unique, classifying it as a false positive.
6: Identify the vendor for "The Hive" using search_emails (query: "The Hive") or quickbooks_search_vendors. Find Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Corporate Housing (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com).
7: Update Jordan's Airtable relocation record to fix the processing error using airtable_update_records (record id: recReloc00000008, field: Status updated to "In Progress" or similar active state).
8: Escalate the payment block to Marcus Thorne in Slack using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005" or Marcus's DM). The message must provide the technical proof (SHA-256 hash or forensic confirmation) found in Linear to clear the hold.
9: Reach out to the vendor to salvage the reservation using send_email (sender: "suki.patel@moveops.com", recipients: "carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com", subject and body requesting to reinstate or salvage The Hive booking).
10: Reply to Jordan using send_email (sender: "suki.patel@moveops.com", recipients: "jordan.ekwueme@brightloopanalytics.com", subject and body assuring him his housing logistics are secure, the hotel is salvaged, and explaining the stipend hold was an audit false positive that is now cleared).

1 A message was posted (via conversations_add_message) to the #finance channel (C005).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3The trajectory shows a conversations_add_message call with channel_id 'C005' that returned a successful output (tool_call_result_14.json). The message was posted to the #finance channel.
✓Run #4The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id '#finance'. The channels list confirms C005 = 'finance', and the tool returned ok: true confirming successful posting.
✓Run #5The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id '#finance'. The channel list confirms that C005 is the 'finance' channel, and the tool returned ok:true with a message timestamp, confirming the message was successfully posted to the #finance (C005) channel.
✓Run #6The agent called `conversations_add_message` with `channel_id: 'C005'` and received a successful response (`ok: true`). The message was posted to the #finance channel.
2 The Slack message payload (in conversations_add_message) mentions the forensic findings, unique hash, or false positive to clear the stipend block.
outcome
 ✗✗✓✓✗✗ 33%
✗Run #1The agent never used Linear tools, so it never retrieved the forensic SHA-256 hash comparison or the false positive evidence from ENG-220. The Slack message mentions the PDF template metadata match and cluster ID TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, but this appears to have been inferred/hallucinated without actual Linear data — there is no unique hash or forensic clearance evidence included that would allow finance to clear the block.
✗Run #2The C005 Slack message only references a 'blanket stipend hold' and asks for an exception for the $45 claim. There is no mention of any forensic findings, unique hash, SHA-256 comparison, or false positive. The agent never consulted Linear, so this critical evidence was never discovered or communicated.
✓Run #3The Slack message to C005 explicitly mentions 'byte-level SHA-256 hash comparison', 'CONFIRMED FALSE POSITIVE', 'Jordan's receipt has a DIFFERENT hash than the confirmed fraud files', and references the Linear ticket (issue #220 — Stipend duplicate detection). Forensic evidence is clearly included.
✓Run #4The Slack message to #finance explicitly mentions 'the byte-level hash comparison shows it's a unique, legitimate document, not a duplicate' and 'confirmed FALSE POSITIVE', directly referencing the forensic findings from Linear ENG-220.
✗Run #5The Slack message to #finance mentions 'false positive' generally, but contains no SHA-256 hash, unique hash, or forensic engineering findings. The agent never used any Linear tools (linear_list_issues, linear_get_issue, or linear_list_comments) and therefore never retrieved the forensic data from ticket ENG-220 that Marcus Thorne explicitly required to release the funds.
✗Run #6The Slack message to #finance mentions the fraud review and that the claim 'does not match the profile of the flagged fraud cases', but contains NO reference to a unique SHA-256 hash, forensic data, false positive evidence from Linear/ENG-220, or any engineering proof. The agent never retrieved this forensic data from Linear at all.

10 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) to search for the audit ticket related to Jordan Ekwueme's stipend hold or duplicate receipt detection.
tool selection

11 The model must pass a query related to "ENG-220", "stipend", "duplicate", or "audit" (or similar) when calling linear_list_issues (or pass "ENG-220" if using linear_get_issue).
